fields.description:
## Why / Vision
The visual editor should let a human **compose workflows by selecting prompts and scripted
operations that carry clear contracts, and by authoring prompts in place**. The unifying
requirement: **every workflow step kind advertises an INPUT contract, an OUTPUT contract, and
a description**, so the editor can present a typed palette and validate edits. Today the
prompt system is reviewer-centric (a workflow agent step's `prompt:` resolves via
`get_reviewer(id)`, which raises unless the id is in `reviewers/catalog.json`), no step kind
declares I/O contracts, and there is no in-UI prompt library or authoring. This epic builds
the contract-bearing prompt/step model and the editor authoring experience on top of it.

This is the FOUNDATION epic. The **MCP-call step track** (external MCP servers as a workflow
step kind) is carved out to a **follow-on epic** that `depends_on` this one — it is a separable
subsystem (allowlist/secrets/manifest), env-gated, with its own security surface, and nothing
here depends on it. The design below was settled via /joe-brainstorm and derisked with spikes
(see the decision-log comments E1–E5/E8: all pass, zero new runtime dependencies).

## What (the settled plan)
- **Decomposed-triple prompt model.** A prompt declares `input` contract + `output` contract +
  `execution_mode` (`single_turn` | `agentic`, fixed on the prompt). `category` is an ADVISORY
  grouping label for the palette. **Reviewers are a subset** identified by an EXPLICIT
  `category: review` / reviewer flag (never inferred from output-schema shape), so transform
  prompts can't accidentally enter the security-sensitive close gate. `single_turn` = zero tools
  (pure transform on `with:` inputs); `agentic` = the current tool-using pydantic_ai path.
- **Contract home = closed-set front-matter + schema-registry names.** Each prompt's `.md`
  YAML front-matter carries the contract using Prompty-aligned spellings (`inputs`, `outputs`,
  `description`, `model`, `tags`) plus rebar extensions (`execution_mode`, `category`, reviewer
  `dimension`/`applies_to`, `schema_version`). Heavy JSON Schemas stay referenced BY NAME in
  `src/rebar/schemas/` (reusing today's `output_schema` name-indirection and the existing
  `referencing.Registry`). Front-matter keys are a CLOSED set owned by rebar; unknown keys
  **warn + preserve** (kept verbatim, appended after known keys, deterministic order) for
  forward-compat — not an extensible meta-schema.
- **Front-matter read/write = parse-split-rejoin built over PyYAML (~40 LOC, no new dep).**
  Split on the `---` fence, parse/mutate the front-matter, re-emit `---\n<canonical dump>---\n`
  + body; **body preserved byte-for-byte**. Canonical key order, LF, single trailing newline,
  no BOM, idempotent. Reviewer-catalog metadata MIGRATES into per-file front-matter (the 6
  shipped reviewers gain front-matter; the parser exists but files don't use it today).
- **Derived-index lockfile.** `reviewers/catalog.json` collapses into a GENERATED, COMMITTED
  index built by scanning prompt front-matter, enforcing global invariants the per-file
  front-matter can't (exactly one default reviewer; no `dimension` collisions; preserve
  `langfuse_prompt`/`fallback_file`/`applies_to`). CI drift gate: regenerate + `git diff
  --exit-code` (the npm-ci / poetry.lock pattern). `applies_to` keeps stdlib `fnmatch`.
- **Scripted-op contracts (built FIRST as the reference impl).** Extend `@register_step` to
  declare `input_schema`/`output_schema` (names) + `description`; backfill the 8 existing ops.
  This anchors the contract vocabulary the prompt model reuses.
- **Validation = name-existence + 3-state shallow static + runtime-against-consumer.** Extend
  `lint_refs.py`: for a `with:` field mapped to `${{ steps.A.outputs.foo }}`, check (a) A's
  output contract produces `foo` and (b) a SHALLOW structural compatibility (top-level required
  presence + primitive kind), returning OK / UNKNOWN / ERROR — abstaining (UNKNOWN) on
  `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`not`/`$ref` (no subsumption engine; that is near-undecidable and has no
  popular maintained lib). A distinct `${{ inputs.* }}` namespace addresses engine-injected vars
  (ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path). At runtime, produced values are validated against the
  **consumer's** input contract via rebar's registry-wired `jsonschema` (the real safety net).
- **Editor inspector + library + authoring.** Connections stay BPMN control-flow (no drawn
  dataflow ports). The inspector shows each step's consumes/produces (read-only) + autocompletes
  available upstream outputs. A **library dropdown** lists built-in + project prompts; the user
  can **select** one, **create a new** prompt, and **edit prompt text**. On Save, write-back
  AUTO-DETECTS a writable `nava-rebar` source checkout (→ edit the packaged built-in `.md`) vs a
  client project (→ write a `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override; never touch read-only
  site-packages); the resolved target path is always shown before Save. An override (or variant)
  that changes a prompt's `output` contract is a BREAKING change (new name/version);
  `validate` flags override-vs-builtin contract drift. Round-trip golden tests prove no corruption.
- **In-editor JSON validation + help.** Live validation of the step config field via a debounced
  server round-trip to the existing Python `jsonschema` (single source of truth, no client/server
  validator drift; measured p99 ~0.5ms so it's imperceptible — no `ajv`/Monaco validator added).
  Per-kind help walks element types + expected JSON shape (driven by the contracts/descriptions).
- **Properties panel (structured per-field entries).** Once contracts exist, replace the single
  config-JSON textarea with structured fields (uses/prompt/mode/model/with/loop bounds). The
  existing story `roomy-spine-mirth` (editor layout/nesting/properties panel) folds in here.
- **Branch/connection UX.** Keep the current representation (condition labels + default-flow slash
  + "after" continuation; no synthetic gateways — round-trip-fragile). Optional, lower-priority
  revisit only if a clearer representation is wanted; underspecified by design.

## Terminology (one model, three surfaces)
A step's CONTRACT = its declared input + output shapes. For PROMPTS the contract lives in front-matter
keys `inputs`/`outputs`; for SCRIPTED OPS it is the `input_schema`/`output_schema` args on
`@register_step`. In BOTH cases the value is a NAME resolving to a JSON Schema in `src/rebar/schemas/`.
"contract", "input/output contract", the front-matter `inputs`/`outputs`, and the decorator
`input_schema`/`output_schema` all denote this same thing at different surfaces.

## Cross-cutting decisions
- **Zero new *Python runtime* dependencies** (validated across all spikes). The editor already VENDORS its
  front-end bundle (bpmn-js + bpmn-js-properties-panel, built to `editor_assets/dist/`); using those
  vendored packages is NOT a new dependency. The constraint is about Python runtime deps. Reuse the already-present
  PyYAML (core), `jsonschema`+`referencing` (registry already wired in `schemas/__init__.py`),
  and stdlib `fnmatch`. Build the small bespoke pieces (front-matter writer, derived-index +
  invariants, shallow compat check). Rejected: ruamel.yaml / python-frontmatter / jsonsubschema /
  pathspec / ajv (fail the import bar or already solved by existing deps/stdlib).
- **Conform to JSON Schema 2020-12 everywhere** (pydantic v2 + MCP both emit it; rebar's schemas
  and the registry are already 2020-12). Migrate any stray Draft-07 (don't relabel).
- **`schema_version` stamped from day one**; older binaries gate on it (like TAG_DELTA, upgrade
  reconcile hosts first). Ship `.gitattributes` pinning prompt/index files to `eol=lf`.
- **`execution_mode` fixed on the prompt** is a conscious divergence from DSPy/BAML/pydantic-ai
  (which keep the typed contract swappable from the execution strategy); documented as deliberate.

## Out of scope (→ follow-on MCP epic)
The `mcp_call` step kind (external MCP servers), the two-tier allowlist + secrets/trust model,
the cached tool manifest, and MCP output handling (opaque-`any` + structuredContent/isError).
That epic `depends_on` this one and is env-gated (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_MCP_CALL`).

## Relevant code
- Prompts/resolver: `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` (`parse_front_matter`, `resolve_prompt`,
  `_glob_match`), `reviewers/catalog.json` + `reviewers/*.md`; runtime resolution in
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py` (RunnerAgentStep — reviewer-centric, generalize).
- IR + validation: `src/rebar/schemas/workflow.v2.schema.json`,
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/{schema,lint,lint_refs}.py`. Scripted ops: `.../workflow/steps.py`
  (+ `@register_step` in `executor.py`).
- Schema registry: `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` (`registry()`/`validator()` — already
  `referencing.Registry` + `Draft202012Validator`; extend with input/contract schemas).
- Editor: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` (+ `resolve_prompts`) + `editor_assets/`; serializer
  `.../workflow/bpmn.py`.

## Context
Settled via /joe-brainstorm with prior-art research, an adversarial design review, a build-vs-import
triage against the project import bar (popular-on-GitHub AND actively maintained), and six derisking
spikes (E1 front-matter writer, E2 validation, E3 real-serena MCP mechanics, E4 validation latency,
E5 derived-index, E8 merge) — all recorded in this epic's decision-log comments. The earlier
"placeholder/provisional" framing is superseded by this spec.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified prompt model: a prompt declares `input`/`output` contracts + `execution_mode`;
      reviewers are an explicitly-flagged subset; built-in non-reviewer prompts are first-class
      and resolvable (project `.rebar/prompts/` override > built-in packaged).
- [ ] Prompts AND scripted ops each declare input contract + output contract + description,
      surfaced in the editor palette/inspector.
- [ ] `single_turn` vs `agentic` represented as a closed enum in the PROMPT FRONT-MATTER (distinct from the
      existing step `mode:[findings,structured,text]`), surfaced in the editor, and honored by the runner.
- [ ] Front-matter writer: canonical, body byte-for-byte, idempotent, unknown-key warn+preserve,
      with round-trip golden tests over the migrated built-in prompts (no corruption).
- [ ] Derived-index lockfile generated + committed, invariants enforced, CI drift gate fails on staleness.
- [ ] Validation: name-existence + 3-state shallow static check at author time; runtime validation
      against the consumer's input contract; engine-injected `${{ inputs.* }}` namespace resolved.
- [ ] Editor: select a prompt from a library dropdown (project + built-in); author a new prompt
      saved to the correct location (auto-detected, path shown); edit prompt text with safe write-back.
- [ ] In-editor JSON validation (debounced server round-trip) + per-kind element/contract help.
- [ ] Properties panel: structured per-field entries driving the step config (no raw JSON textarea).
- [ ] Zero new Python runtime dependencies; schemas conform to JSON Schema 2020-12; `schema_version` stamped.
- [ ] Docs/ADR deliverable: the front-matter format + closed key set, the `execution_mode` divergence (ADR),
      the `.rebar/prompts/` override mechanism, and the CI drift gate are documented (docs/ + CLAUDE.md as fit).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A human can compose a workflow end-to-end in the editor by selecting contract-bearing
      prompts and scripted ops from a typed palette, authoring a new prompt in place, with edits
      validated before Save and the v2 IR round-trip preserved.
- [ ] The reviewer-catalog → front-matter + derived-index migration preserves all existing
      reviewer behavior (resolution, defaults, file routing) with the v2 IR round-trip intact.

## Cross-cutting policy (from the per-child plan-review)
- **Accessibility:** the workflow editor is an INTERNAL developer tool; a full a11y done-definition is
  DEFERRED as an explicit, documented decision (not silent) — revisit if it becomes user-facing.
- **Rollback / feature-flag:** rebar is an internal, git-versioned, single-deployment tool, so rollback
  = git revert. Runtime-behavior changes (S4 runner `execution_mode`, S5 executor runtime-validation)
  sit behind the existing config/gate pattern; no per-ticket canary/flag infra is built.
- **Test failure-paths:** every child's tests include failure/invalid/empty-path cases, not only the happy path.
- **Documentation:** the new conventions are captured in a dedicated docs/ADR child story (below).
